text.title: EVP Examples by Company Type
text.summary: Three worked EVP examples (tech startup, remote-first, mission-driven) showing statement and pillar breakdown.

example.note: Early-stage B2B SaaS, 50 employees. Wins on impact and growth; competes on equity.
example.code:
EVP Statement: "For ambitious engineers who want to make an outsized impact, TechCo offers the rare combination of startup ownership with scale-up stability. Build products used by millions while growing your career faster than at any BigCo."
Pillar 1 — Impact: own features end-to-end, ship weekly Proof points: direct Jira ownership per engineer, weekly releases, zero approval layers Pillar 2 — Growth: engineering ladder, $5K L&D budget Proof points: published IC1-IC6 ladder doc, documented L&D policy, 3 internal promotions last year Pillar 3 — Equity: meaningful options, not tokens Proof points: 0.1-0.5% grants at IC3+, standard 4-year/1-year cliff, published strike prices

example.note: Fully remote, 200 employees across 30 countries. Wins on flexibility and inclusion.
example.code:
EVP Statement: "For professionals who believe great work happens anywhere, RemoteCo provides true location independence with the connection of a tight-knit team. Work from Bali or Baltimore — your impact matters, not your timezone."
Pillar 1 — Flexibility: work from anywhere, async-first Proof points: no required meeting hours, home-office stipend $2K/year, overlap hours 2h/day max Pillar 2 — Connection: quarterly meetups, strong culture Proof points: 4 team retreats/year, Donut coffee-chat program, Culture Amp score 4.3 Pillar 3 — Inclusion: equal pay globally, diverse team Proof points: location-adjusted salary bands published internally, 43% non-US employees

example.note: Climate tech company. Wins on purpose and innovation; accepts below-market comp.
example.code:
EVP Statement: "For those who want their work to matter, ClimateCo offers the rare chance to build technology that directly fights climate change. Join scientists and engineers who chose impact over increments."
Pillar 1 — Purpose: direct climate impact Proof points: 12 MtCO2e avoided (verifiable, published), B-Corp certified, mission KPIs shared quarterly Pillar 2 — Innovation: cutting-edge clean tech Proof points: 3 patents filed 2024, $2M R&D budget, 6 PhDs on team Pillar 3 — Community: work with passionate people Proof points: Glassdoor 4.6, eNPS +52, avg tenure 3.1 years (above sector median)
